2604.25602.title: OxyGent: Making Multi-Agent Systems Modular, Observable, and Evolvable via Oxy Abstraction
2604.25602.paper_summary: Deploying production-ready multi-agent systems (MAS) in complex industrial environments remains challenging due to limitations in scalability, observability, and autonomous evolution. We present OxyGent, an open-source framework that enables modular, observable, and evolvable MAS via a unified Oxy abstraction, in which agents, tools, LLMs, and reasoning flows are encapsulated as pluggable atomic components. This Lego-like assembly paradigm supports scalable system composition and non-intrusive monitoring. To enhance observability, OxyGent introduces permission-driven dynamic planning that replaces rigid workflows with execution graphs generated at runtime, which provide adaptive visualizations. To support continuous evolution, the framework integrates OxyBank, an AI asset management platform that supports automated data backflow, annotation, and joint evolution. Empirical evaluations and real-world case studies show that OxyGent provides a robust and scalable foundation for MAS. OxyGent is publicly available at https://oxygent.jd.com/.
2604.25602.paper_summary_zh: 在複雜的工業環境中，部署生產就緒的多代理系統（MAS）仍然面臨挑戰，原因在於可擴展性、可觀察性和自主演化的限制。<lb/>我們提出了OxyGent，一個開源框架，通過統一的Oxy抽象，使模組化、可觀察和可演化的MAS成為可能，其中代理、工具、LLMs和推理流程被封裝為可插拔的原子組件。<lb/>這種類樂高的組裝範式支持可擴展的系統組合和非侵入式監控。<lb/>為了增強可觀察性，OxyGent引入了基於權限的動態規劃，這種規劃用在運行時生成的執行圖替代了僵化的工作流程，從而提供自適應的可視化。<lb/>為了支持持續演化，該框架集成了OxyBank，一個支持自動數據回流、標註和聯合演化的AI資產管理平台。<lb/>實證評估和現實案例研究表明，OxyGent為MAS提供了一個穩健且可擴展的基礎。<lb/>OxyGent可在https://oxygent.jd.com/上公開獲得。
2604.25602.author: Junxing Hu et.al.
2604.25602.authors: Junxing Hu, Tianlong Li, Lei Yu, Ai Han
